moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for income and broad index stability.
Buy XLK for 10% to add a quality tech name.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall risk.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 15-20%.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a ~1.5% monthly return with a focus on capital preservation. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by eliminating leveraged ETFs and reducing concentration. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns.

moves.note: Add quality tech with strong scanner

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to remove leveraged ETF risk.
Increase QQQI to 20% for income and stability.
Add to SMH to 18% and buy XLK for 10% to blend quality tech growth.
This plan balances growth potential with reduced risk.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 10%.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5% monthly growth by blending broad index and quality tech. It reduces leverage and concentration while maintaining exposure to strong setups. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns.

moves.note: Add strong tech setup, good R/R

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Add to SOXL to 10% to lean into leveraged semiconductors.
Trim QQQI to 10% to shift focus from income to growth.
Increase SMH to 20% and CRWD to 15% to press strong tech sectors.
Buy XLK for 10% to add a high-scoring tech setup.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer of approximately 5%.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by using leverage and concentrating in high-scoring setups. It significantly raises both upside and drawdown potential. Be aware that leveraged ETFs decay daily, and this approach can lose a lot fast if stops are not strictly adhered to.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize income generation.
Trim QQQ to 10% and SMH to 5% to reduce growth and high-beta exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% monthly distributions by rotating into income and broad index ETFs. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth. Remember that distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
